If Margot's state is still conveyed only through 'sleeping badly,' skipped meals, hallway-walking, or an awkward line break, this finding remains unresolved.

findings.acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:15 and at least one later Margot beat around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:167 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:185, a cold reader can identify one explicit bodily symptom of feed withdrawal and one visible behavior caused by it. If Margot's state is still conveyed only through 'sleeping badly,' skipped meals, hallway-walking, or an awkward line break, this finding remains unresolved.
findings.problem: Margot's post-feed state is rendered as generic suspension malaise instead of a specific addiction aftermath. The chapter gives her bad sleep, corridor-walking, skipped meals, and an awkward farewell, but not the bodily craving or sensory flatness that the novel's risk policy makes central to her condition. Because the chapter is the last time she shares space with the group before Doha, this vagueness reads as sanitization rather than restraint.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the Margot material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:81, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:185 so the chapter shows what feed deprivation does to her body now, not just what her schedule looks like. Keep the scene quiet, but add concrete withdrawal evidence inside the opening summary and/or the cleanup goodbye: a specific phantom sensation, compulsion, deadened baseline, or physical coping behavior tied to losing the feed. Do not substitute alcohol-adjacent self-policing or generalized restlessness for the actual addiction consequence.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised lines around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:107 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:115, Kai still communicates that his mother detects a difference between earlier and enhanced versions of his running, but a side-by-side read against the style bible would no longer show a near-verbatim or near-structural echo of the calibration example. If the revised passage still depends on the same 'old footage' plus 'she can tell the difference' setup, this finding fails.
findings.problem: Kai's conversation about his mother and the old race clips echoes a calibration-only style-bible example too closely: the same mother/old footage/natural version/tell-the-difference scaffold reappears in a central chapter beat. That damages voice integrity because the scene reads like reference material being reused instead of Kai discovering fresh language in the moment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:107, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:111, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:115 with new sentence rhythm, new image selection, and a different conversational route into the same dramatic point. Preserve the underlying function that Kai's mother notices a pre-enhancement version of him and that he half-believes her, but avoid the old-footage/natural-category/tell-the-difference phrasing pattern entirely.

findings.acceptance_test: Lines 3428-3441 either omit the mother-footage story or present a materially new development in the mother's reaction. The phrase 'she can tell the difference' does not appear.

findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3428-3441, either cut the mother-footage anecdote entirely (the reader knows it from Ch 7) or advance it: the mother has sent something new, her reaction has changed, or Kai's relationship to the footage has shifted. evidence.excerpt: 3326: The facility emptied in stages. Those who'd signed Meridian's contract left first — patched, data-licensed, their departures arranged through corporate travel. A sprinter from the Czech team. The pair of gymnasts from South Korea. The relay group, all four of them, boarding the shuttle to Zurich on the same Tuesday morning with matching duffel bags and matching firmware. Each departure thinned the cafeteria by a face, the morning track sessions by a body. By late June the residential wing was half-dark, most of the room displays glowing their real-time diagnostics to nobody.

3330: Kai ran nothing. He trained on the indoor track alone most mornings, running two hundreds in the facility's thin air, the rubber smell and the echo of his footfalls the same as they'd been in January except the light through the high windows was different now — brighter, longer, actual daylight instead of the winter's grey approximation. His wrist ached on cold mornings, the bone where the break had knitted too fast — a shallow wrongness, like the memory of an injury rather than the injury itself. He'd stopped wearing the brace weeks ago but the ache stayed. He ran with it. He ran paying attention to something most people couldn't feel: the synthetic muscle's autonomous subsystems, the tiny pre-movements in his quadriceps and calves that fired fractionally ahead of his conscious intent. He'd been training himself to notice the gap between what he told his legs to do and what they did first. He did not call this practice anything. He just ran, and paid attention, and ran again.

3332: Lena swam. The pool was less crowded now, which was its own kind of strange — she'd trained in packed lanes her entire life, and the empty lanes beside her made the water feel wider and flatter, as though something had been subtracted from it. Her times were slower than her optimization peak by margins that would have been unacceptable six months ago. She swam them anyway. The partial patch held. The silence in her head where the system's ambient processing had been was becoming, if not comfortable, then at least recognizable — a space she could orient herself inside of, the way your eyes adjusted to a room after someone turned the light off.

3334: Yusuf worked. He gave the partial patch to two more athletes in the third week — a hurdler from Jamaica and a pole vaulter from New Zealand, both holdouts who'd decided the risk of competing unpatched outweighed the risk of an untested firmware rewrite. The installations went smoothly. Both athletes reported the same things Lena had: blunted sensory acuity, slower reaction times, and a new quiet in their heads that they described in different words but that Yusuf recognized as the same phenomenon. He filed reports. He calibrated the remaining athletes on their adjusted schedules and answered technical questions from the Davos satellite team over encrypted channels. He called Farah on Thursdays and listened to her talk about the card game she was winning, her voice the same warm voice it had always been, her speech just slightly slower than it used to be in a way that he measured against last week and the week before that the way he measured everything — in increments, in data, in the precise language of decline.

3336: Margot walked the corridors. She had nowhere to be and nowhere else to go. Her coaching suspension was total — no feed access, no training supervision, no athlete contact in any professional capacity. She'd been offered a transfer to Meridian's administrative center in Zurich, which she understood correctly as a polite suggestion that she leave, and she'd declined it with three words. She still had her quarters. She still ate in the cafeteria. She still walked the corridors of the facility in the mornings and evenings, her body moving through spaces that had been defined by her work and were now just hallways. She was sleeping badly. She'd taken to running the outdoor loop around the facility in the late afternoons — three kilometers of gravel path that switchbacked down to the treeline and back up, the altitude making her lungs burn in a way she didn't mind because it was at least a sensation she'd chosen. She was not drinking, which was something she noticed herself noticing, because it would have been easy and because the facility bar in the common room stayed open until eleven and nobody was watching.

findings.problem: After fifteen chapters of granular scene work, Chapter 16 opens with four paragraphs of montage summary covering four weeks of departures, individual character updates, and the facility emptying. Athletes who signed Meridian's contract 'left first' (line 3326); the holdouts are described in a single paragraph (3328); Kai, Lena, Yusuf, and Margot each receive one summary paragraph of their four-week experience (3330-3337). This is telling at scale — the departures, the thinning facility, the emotional weight of colleagues leaving — none are rendered as lived scenes. The shift from the novel's established close-third POV texture to omniscient montage is abrupt. The biryani dinner that follows is well-executed, but the opening summary reads as connective tissue rather than inhabited fiction.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3322-3340, convert at least one departure into a brief scene (a goodbye at the shuttle, a room going dark, a name disappearing from the hallway display). The character summaries at lines 3330-3337 can stay compressed but should include at least one specific scene-moment per character rather than pure summary. Margot's paragraph (3336) is the strongest of the four because it includes concrete detail (the outdoor loop, the bar). Bring the others to that specificity level.

findings.acceptance_test: The biryani scene (Ch 16) contains one port-touch gesture, not two.

evidence.excerpt: 3466: Lena had been quiet for a few minutes. She was looking out the window at the mountains and the last of the light. Her hand went to the back of her neck, where the neural link port sat under her hair. She touched it and brought her hand back down, and it was unclear whether the gesture was deliberate or the unconscious check Kai had seen her make dozens of times.

3496: Lena stayed at the table. She was looking at the dark windows where the mountains had been. Her hand went to the back of her neck again and she left it there this time, her fingers resting against the small circle of the port.

findings.problem: The montage opening re-teaches each character's condition: Kai's proprioception-gap practice (3330, established in Ch 7), Lena's system silence (3332, established in Ch 13), Yusuf's patch installations and Farah's decline (3334, established in Ch 3, 12, 13). Each summary paragraph re-delivers information the reader has.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3330-3337, each character paragraph should show the four-week state through a specific moment rather than summarizing the condition. Kai: a moment on the track where the gap is different. Lena: a specific morning in the pool where the silence is easier. Yusuf: a specific Thursday call with Farah. These micro-scenes replace the summaries.

findings.problem: Ch 16 has em-dash density of 25.9%, far exceeding the 10% threshold. The em-dash is used for parenthetical asides, appositives, and mid-sentence pivots at a rate that flattens the prose rhythm. Manuscript target: reduce from 654 to <150 em-dash lines.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 16 from 28 to ~9 (19 cuts). Convert parenthetical em-dash asides to commas, semicolons, colons, periods, or restructured sentences. Reserve em-dashes for their highest-impact uses: dramatic interruptions, voice-specific rhythms, and moments where the break creates meaning.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 16 has no more than 16 sentences (≤7% of ~236) beginning with 'She.'
findings.problem: Ch 16 has 'She'-opener density of 12.3%, well above the 7% threshold. This creates a rhythmic monotony where consecutive sentences beginning with 'She' flatten the prose into a subject-verb march.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'She'-opener sentences in Ch 16 from 29 to ~16 (13 rewrites). Vary sentence openings: lead with setting, sensory detail, dependent clauses, object-first constructions, or dialogue attributions.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
